title: QTLs
text: , use GWAS-eQTL colocalization results provided via echolocatoR Fine-mapping Portal, publication: Lopes, K.d.P., Snijders, G.J.L., Humphrey, J. et al. Genetic analysis human microglial transcriptome across brain regions, aging disease pathologies. Nat Genet 54, 4–17 (2022). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41588-021-00976-y

title: QTLs
text: data actually merged GWAS-QTL colocalization results, contains necessary columns original eQTL summary stats need perform eQTL fine-mapping.
code: coloc_res <- echodata::get_Kunkle2019_coloc(return_path = TRUE)

title: QTLs
text: Prepare column mapping object summary statistics. ’ll reuse import_topSNPs finemap_loci steps.
code: colmap <- echodata::construct_colmap(       CHR = "chr",       POS = "pos",       N = "qtl.N",       SNP = "snp",       P = "qtl.pvalues",       Effect = "qtl.beta",       StdErr = "qtl.varbeta",       MAF = "qtl.MAF",       Locus = "Locus",       Gene = "gene")

title: QTLs
text: case, don’t top SNPs file ready. ’re just going make one directly full summary stats file (NOTE: can use approach can fit entire file memory). case, ’ll want make sure set grouping_vars=c("Locus","Gene") get top SNPs eGene-locus pair (just one SNP per locus).

title: QTLs
text: following call runs full fine-mapping pipeline QTL data. computationally expensive may take 10–30 minutes per locus.
code: res <- echolocatoR::finemap_loci(fullSS_path = coloc_res$path,                                  topSNPs = topSNPs,                                  ## Let's just fine-map 1 locus for demo purposes                                  loci = topSNPs$Locus[1],                                  dataset_name = "Kunkle_2019.microgliaQTL",                                  dataset_type = "QTL",                                  bp_distance = 1000,                                  colmap = colmap,                                  show_plot = TRUE,                                  finemap_methods = c("ABF","FINEMAP","SUSIE") )

title: QTLs
text: Explore interpret results: vignette("explore_results") Visualize loci: vignette("plotting") Summarise across loci: vignette("summarise") GWAS fine-mapping (non-QTL): vignette("echolocatoR")

title: Docker/Singularity Containers
text: echolocator now available via DockerHub containerised environment Rstudio necessary dependencies pre-installed.

title: Docker/Singularity Containers
text: First, install Docker already. Create image Docker container command line: image created, can launch :
code: docker pull bschilder/echolocator docker run \   -d \   -e ROOT=true \   -e PASSWORD="<your_password>" \   -v ~/Desktop:/Desktop \   -v /Volumes:/Volumes \   -p 8787:8787 \   bschilder/echolocator

title: Docker/Singularity Containers
text: Make sure replace <your_password> whatever want password . Change paths supplied -v flags particular use case. -d ensures container run “detached” mode, means persist even ’ve closed command line session. username “rstudio” default. Optionally, can also install Docker Desktop easily manage containers.

title: Docker/Singularity Containers
text: using system allow Docker (case many institutional computing clusters), can instead install Docker images via Singularity.
code: singularity pull docker://bschilder/echolocator

title: Docker/Singularity Containers
text: Finally, launch containerised Rstudio entering following URL web browser: http://localhost:8787/ Login using credentials set Installation steps.

text: examples use data Parkinson’s disease GWAS Nalls et al. (2019).

title: Get started
text: enable rapid fine-mapping many loci, can create top_SNPs data.frame contains position lead/index SNP within locus. finemap_loci() (see next step) use info extract subsets full GWAS/QTL summary statistics using windows centered lead/index SNP. topSS argument can either data.frame, path topSS file saved somewhere. common tabular data formats (e.g. .tsv, .csv, .xlsx) accepted.

title: Get started
text: Since full GWAS summary stats file large include within echolocatoR, instead provide example subset full summary stats. simulate ’d actually use full summary stats file, save example dataset computer (can change path wherever like). highly recommend munging full summary stats using Bioconductor package MungeSumstats first. ’s easy use robust. also means don’t provide column mapping arguments finemap_loci munged=TRUE. ’s example munge full summary stats file: already munged following example summary stats .
code: fullSS_path <- echodata::example_fullSS(munged = FALSE) fullSS_path <- MungeSumstats::format_sumstats(path = fullSS_path, ref_genome = "GRCH37") fullSS_path <- echodata::example_fullSS(dataset = "Nalls2019") ## Writing file to ==> /tmp/RtmplzYLji/nalls2019.fullSS_subset.tsv

title: Get started
text: full description arguments, see ?finemap_loci. key arguments: results_dir: want store results. finemap_methods: fine-mapping methods want run. full list currently supported methods, run function echofinemap::lfm(). bp_distance: Controls window size. Specifically, bp_distance number basepairs upstream/downstream want extract locus. example, want 2Mb window (+/- 1Mb lead/index SNP top_SNPs), set bp_distance=1e+06. plot_zoom: Zoom /center locus producing multiview plot. can adjust separately bp_distance don’t rerun whole pipeline time (locus subsets, LD matrices, fine-mapping results automatically saved locus-specific folders). Note: Please use full absolute paths (instead relative paths) wherever possible (e.g. results_dir). especially important tool FINEMAP. following call fine-maps two loci (BST1 MEX3C) using three statistical fine-mapping methods. Depending machine internet speed, may take 10-30 minutes per locus.
code: results <- echolocatoR::finemap_loci(  fullSS_path = fullSS_path,  topSNPs = topSNPs,  loci = c("BST1","MEX3C"),  LD_reference = "1KGphase3",  dataset_name = "Nalls23andMe_2019",  fullSS_genome_build = "hg19",  bp_distance = 1000,  finemap_methods = c("ABF","SUSIE","FINEMAP"),  munged = TRUE)

title: Get started
text: output finemap_loci() named list data.tables, one per locus. data.table contains original GWAS summary statistics augmented fine-mapping posterior probabilities credible set assignments. use bundled BST1 example results show output looks like: Consensus fine-mapped SNPs BST1 locus
code: dat <- echodata::BST1 ## Top fine-mapped SNPs (consensus across methods) consensus <- subset(dat, Consensus_SNP == TRUE) knitr::kable(     consensus[, c("SNP","CHR","POS","P","mean.PP","Support","Consensus_SNP")],     caption = "Consensus fine-mapped SNPs for the BST1 locus",     row.names = FALSE )

title: Get started
text: Explore interpret results: vignette("explore_results") Visualize loci: vignette("plotting") Summarise across loci: vignette("summarise") Fine-map QTL data: vignette("QTLs") Learn sub-packages: vignette("echoverse_modules")

title: echoverse modules
text: echolocatoR orchestrates suite modular R packages collectively called echoverse. echolocatoR::finemap_loci() runs full pipeline automatically, module can also used independently custom workflows. vignette provides quick reference use module.

title: echoverse modules
text: provide foundational utilities used modules.

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: Data management, example datasets, column standardization. Use independently want : Load bundled fine-mapping results: BST1, LRRK2, MEX3C Download full GWAS summary stats: get_Nalls2019(), get_Kunkle2019() Standardize column names across different GWAS formats Access Fine-Mapping Portal: portal_query()
code: ## Bundled fine-mapping results (no download needed) dat <- echodata::BST1 cat("BST1 locus:", nrow(dat), "SNPs,", ncol(dat), "columns\n") ## BST1 locus: 6216 SNPs, 26 columns

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: GitHub API utilities downloading files releases. Use independently want : Download files GitHub repos programmatically Access GitHub release assets

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: File download helpers caching. Use independently want : Download cache large files progress tracking Upload data Zenodo

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: Developer utilities echoverse packages.

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: Tabix-indexed file querying liftover. Use independently want : Query tabix-indexed VCF summary stats files genomic region Perform genome build liftover (hg19/hg38) Convert file formats

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: LD (linkage disequilibrium) matrix computation. Use independently want : Compute LD matrices 1000 Genomes Phase 3, UK Biobank, custom VCFs Filter variants LD MAF thresholds
code: ## Bundled LD matrix ld <- echodata::BST1_LD_matrix cat("LD matrix:", nrow(ld), "x", ncol(ld), "SNPs\n") ## LD matrix: 95 x 95 SNPs

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: Conda environment management external tools. Use independently want : Set conda environments FINEMAP, PolyFun, command-line tools Manage Python dependencies R via basilisk

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: Statistical fine-mapping methods. Use independently want : Run individual fine-mapping methods (ABF, SUSIE, FINEMAP, PolyFun+SUSIE) Compare results across methods See available methods: echofinemap::lfm()
code: ## List available fine-mapping methods echofinemap::lfm() ## Gathering method sources. ## Gathering method citations. ## [1] "ABF"              "COJO_conditional" "COJO_joint"       "COJO_stepwise"    ## [5] "FINEMAP"          "PAINTOR"          "POLYFUN_FINEMAP"  "POLYFUN_SUSIE"    ## [9] "SUSIE"

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: Functional annotation enrichment. Use independently want : Overlap fine-mapped SNPs ROADMAP epigenomic annotations Run GoShifter enrichment tests Annotate NOTT2019/CORCES2020 single-cell data Create super summary plots

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: Locus visualization. Use independently want : Create multi-track locus zoom plots Visualize fine-mapping posterior probabilities alongside GWAS p-values Overlay LD, gene tracks, annotations

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: QTL colocalization via eQTL Catalogue. Use independently want : Query eQTL Catalogue colocalization analysis Access tissue/cell-type-specific QTL datasets

title: echoverse modules
text: Purpose: Unified fine-mapping pipeline. package ’re using now. calls modules correct order via finemap_loci() / finemap_locus().

title: Exploring fine-mapping results
text: vignette demonstrates explore interpret fine-mapping results produced finemap_loci(). examples use bundled data included echodata, internet connection required.

title: Exploring fine-mapping results
text: echodata ships pre-computed fine-mapping results three Parkinson’s disease loci Nalls et al. (2019): row SNP within locus window. Key columns include: GWAS columns: SNP, CHR, POS, P, Effect, StdErr, MAF Fine-mapping posterior probabilities: ABF.PP, SUSIE.PP, FINEMAP.PP Credible set assignments: ABF.CS, SUSIE.CS, FINEMAP.CS Consensus columns: Support (number methods placing SNP CS), Consensus_SNP, mean.PP, mean.CS Top 10 SNPs mean posterior probability
code: dat <- echodata::BST1 dim(dat) ## [1] 6216   26 ## Fine-mapping specific columns fm_cols <- c("SNP","P","ABF.PP","SUSIE.PP","FINEMAP.PP",              "Support","Consensus_SNP","mean.PP") knitr::kable(head(dat[order(dat$mean.PP, decreasing = TRUE), ..fm_cols], 10),              digits = 4, row.names = FALSE,              caption = "Top 10 SNPs by mean posterior probability")

title: Exploring fine-mapping results
text: Consensus SNPs placed credible set multiple fine-mapping methods. Support column indicates many methods agree: Consensus fine-mapped SNPs (BST1 locus)
code: consensus <- subset(dat, Consensus_SNP == TRUE) knitr::kable(     consensus[, c("SNP","POS","P","ABF.PP","SUSIE.PP","FINEMAP.PP",                    "Support","mean.PP")],     digits = 4, row.names = FALSE,     caption = "Consensus fine-mapped SNPs (BST1 locus)" )

title: Exploring fine-mapping results
text: Summarise many SNPs fall support level: SNP counts support level
code: support_table <- table(Support = dat$Support) knitr::kable(as.data.frame(support_table),              caption = "SNP counts by support level")

title: Exploring fine-mapping results
text: fine-mapping method assigns SNPs credible sets (CS). value 0 means SNP CS; 1 = first CS, 2 = second CS, etc. Number SNPs credible sets per method
code: ## Count SNPs in each method's credible sets cs_summary <- data.frame(     Method = c("ABF", "SUSIE", "FINEMAP"),     SNPs_in_CS = c(         sum(dat$ABF.CS > 0, na.rm = TRUE),         sum(dat$SUSIE.CS > 0, na.rm = TRUE),         sum(dat$FINEMAP.CS > 0, na.rm = TRUE)     ) ) knitr::kable(cs_summary, caption = "Number of SNPs in credible sets per method")

title: Exploring fine-mapping results
text: echodata also bundles LRRK2 MEX3C loci. can combine simulate multi-locus analysis: Summary across bundled loci
code: bst1 <- data.table::copy(echodata::BST1); bst1[, Locus := "BST1"] lrrk2 <- data.table::copy(echodata::LRRK2); lrrk2[, Locus := "LRRK2"] mex3c <- data.table::copy(echodata::MEX3C); mex3c[, Locus := "MEX3C"] shared_cols <- Reduce(intersect, list(colnames(bst1), colnames(lrrk2), colnames(mex3c))) all_loci <- rbind(bst1[, ..shared_cols], lrrk2[, ..shared_cols], mex3c[, ..shared_cols]) ## Consensus SNPs per locus consensus_per_locus <- do.call(rbind, lapply(     split(all_loci, all_loci$Locus),     function(x) data.frame(         Locus = x$Locus[1],         Total_SNPs = nrow(x),         Consensus_SNPs = sum(x$Consensus_SNP, na.rm = TRUE),         Min_P = min(x$P, na.rm = TRUE)     ) )) knitr::kable(consensus_per_locus, row.names = FALSE,              caption = "Summary across bundled loci")

title: Exploring fine-mapping results
text: pre-computed LD matrix BST1 also available: LD matrix (top-left corner)
code: ld <- echodata::BST1_LD_matrix cat("LD matrix dimensions:", dim(ld), "\n") ## LD matrix dimensions: 95 95 cat("SNPs in LD matrix:", ncol(ld), "\n") ## SNPs in LD matrix: 95 ## Show a small corner knitr::kable(round(ld[1:5, 1:5], 3), caption = "LD matrix (top-left corner)")

title: Plotting fine-mapping results
text: echoplot provides multi-track locus plots combine GWAS association signals, fine-mapping posterior probabilities, LD structure, gene models single figure. vignette demonstrates common plotting workflows using bundled example data — internet connection required. full fine-mapping pipeline, see vignette("echolocatoR"). interpreting results, see vignette("explore_results").

title: Plotting fine-mapping results
code: dat <- echodata::BST1 LD_matrix <- echodata::BST1_LD_matrix locus_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), echodata::locus_dir)

title: Plotting fine-mapping results
text: main function echoplot::plot_locus() creates multi-panel figure GWAS p-values, fine-mapping posterior probabilities, gene tracks. SNPs colored LD r² lead SNP.
code: plt <- tryCatch(     echoplot::plot_locus(         dat = dat,         locus_dir = locus_dir,         LD_matrix = LD_matrix,         show_plot = FALSE,         save_plot = FALSE,         verbose = FALSE     ),     error = function(e) { message("plot_locus error: ", e$message); NULL } ) ## + support_thresh = 2 ## + Calculating mean Posterior Probability (mean.PP)... ## + 4 fine-mapping methods used. ## + 7 Credible Set SNPs identified. ## + 3 Consensus SNPs identified. ## + Filling NAs in CS cols with 0. ## + Filling NAs in PP cols with 0. ## Loading required namespace: ggrepel ## Loading required namespace: EnsDb.Hsapiens.v75 ## Fetching data...OK ## Parsing exons...OK ## Defining introns...OK ## Defining UTRs...OK ## Defining CDS...OK ## aggregating... ## Done ## Constructing graphics... ## + echoplot:: Get window suffix... if (!is.null(plt)) plt[["1x"]]

title: Plotting fine-mapping results
text: can generate plots different zoom levels focus fine-mapped region:
code: plt_zoom <- tryCatch(     echoplot::plot_locus(         dat = dat,         locus_dir = locus_dir,         LD_matrix = LD_matrix,         zoom = c("1x", "4x"),         show_plot = FALSE,         save_plot = FALSE,         verbose = FALSE     ),     error = function(e) { message("plot_locus error: ", e$message); NULL } ) ## + support_thresh = 2 ## + Calculating mean Posterior Probability (mean.PP)... ## + 4 fine-mapping methods used. ## + 7 Credible Set SNPs identified. ## + 3 Consensus SNPs identified. ## + Filling NAs in CS cols with 0. ## + Filling NAs in PP cols with 0. ## Fetching data...OK ## Parsing exons...OK ## Defining introns...OK ## Defining UTRs...OK ## Defining CDS...OK ## aggregating... ## Done ## Constructing graphics... ## + echoplot:: Get window suffix... ## + echoplot:: Get window suffix... ## + Constructing zoom polygon... ## + Highlighting zoom origin... if (!is.null(plt_zoom)) plt_zoom[["4x"]]

title: Plotting fine-mapping results
text: don’t LD matrix, plot still works — SNPs shown without r² coloring:
code: plt_nold <- tryCatch(     echoplot::plot_locus(         dat = dat,         locus_dir = locus_dir,         LD_matrix = NULL,         show_plot = FALSE,         save_plot = FALSE,         verbose = FALSE     ),     error = function(e) { message("plot_locus error: ", e$message); NULL } ) ## + support_thresh = 2 ## + Calculating mean Posterior Probability (mean.PP)... ## + 4 fine-mapping methods used. ## + 7 Credible Set SNPs identified. ## + 3 Consensus SNPs identified. ## + Filling NAs in CS cols with 0. ## + Filling NAs in PP cols with 0. ## Fetching data...OK ## Parsing exons...OK ## Defining introns...OK ## Defining UTRs...OK ## Defining CDS...OK ## aggregating... ## Done ## Constructing graphics... ## + echoplot:: Get window suffix... if (!is.null(plt_nold)) plt_nold[["1x"]]

title: Plotting fine-mapping results
text: save plot disk:
code: plt <- echoplot::plot_locus(     dat = dat,     locus_dir = locus_dir,     LD_matrix = LD_matrix,     save_plot = TRUE,     plot_format = "png",     dpi = 300,     height = 12,     width = 10,     show_plot = FALSE )

title: Automated Genomic Fine-Mapping
text: Fine-mapping methods powerful means identifying causal variants underlying given phenotype, underutilized due technical challenges implementation. echolocatoR R package automates end--end genomics fine-mapping, annotation, plotting order identify probable causal variants associated given phenotype. requires minimal input users (GWAS QTL summary statistics file), includes suite statistical functional fine-mapping tools. also includes extensive access datasets (linkage disequilibrium panels, epigenomic genome-wide annotations, QTL).

title: Automated Genomic Fine-Mapping
text: use echolocatoR, echoverse modules, please cite: Brian M Schilder, Jack Humphrey, Towfique Raj (2021). echolocatoR: automated end--end statistical functional genomic fine-mapping pipeline. Bioinformatics, btab658. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btab658 elimination data gathering preprocessing steps enables rapid fine-mapping many loci phenotype, complete locus-specific publication-ready figure generation. results merged single per-SNP summary file additional downstream analysis results sharing. Therefore echolocatoR drastically reduces barriers identifying causal variants making entire fine-mapping pipeline rapid, robust scalable.

title: Automated Genomic Fine-Mapping
text: series major updates echolocatoR v1.0 v2.0. notable ones (see Details): echoverse subpackages: echolocatoR broken separate subpackages, making much easier edit/debug step full finemap_loci pipeline, improving robustness throughout. also provides greater flexibility users construct custom pipelines modules. GITHUB_TOKEN: GitHub now requires users create Personal Authentication Tokens (PAT) avoid download limits. essential installing echolocatoR many resources GitHub need downloaded. See instructions. = echodata::construct_colmap(): Previously, users required input key column name mappings separate arguments echolocatoR::finemap_loci. functionality deprecated replaced single argument, colmap=. allows users save construct_colmap() output single variable reuse later without write mapping argument (helps reduce already crowded list arguments). MungeSumstats: finemap_loci now accepts output MungeSumstats::format_sumstats/import_sumstats -(without requiring colmap=, long munged=TRUE). Standardizing GWAS/QTL summary stats way greatly reduces (eliminates) time taken manual formatting. echolocatoR::finemap_loci arguments: Several arguments deprecated names changed harmonized across subpackages use unified naming convention. See ?echolocatoR::finemap_loci details. echoconda: echoverse subpackage echoconda now handles conda environment creation/use internally automatically, without need users create conda environment separate step. Also, default conda env echoR replaced echoR_mini, reduces number dependencies just bare minimum (thus greatly speeding build time reducing potential version conflicts). FINEMAP: outputs tool FINEMAP now recorded echolocatoR results (see ?echofinemap::FINEMAP Issue details). Also, common dependency conflict FINEMAP>=1.4 MacOS resolved (see Issue details. echodata: example data data transformation functions moved echoverse subpackage echodata. LD_reference=: addition UKB, 1KGphase1/3 LD reference panels, finemap_loci() can now take custom LD panels supplying finemap_loci(LD_reference=) list paths VCF files (.vcf / vcf.gz / vcf.bgz) pre-computed LD matrices RSIDs row/col names (.rda / .rds / .csv / .tsv. / .txt / .csv.gz / tsv.gz / txt.gz). Expanded fine-mapping methods: “ABF”,”COJO_conditional”, “COJO_joint” “COJO_stepwise”,“FINEMAP”,“PAINTOR” (including multi-GWAS multi-ancestry fine-mapping),“POLYFUN_FINEMAP” ,“POLYFUN_SUSIE”,“SUSIE” FINEMAP fixed: number issues FINEMAP due differing output formats across different versions, system dependency conflicts, fact can produce multiple Credible Sets. fixed latest version FINEMAP can run OS platforms. Debug mode: Within finemap_loci() use tryCatch() iterating across loci one locus fails, rest can continue. However prevents using traceback feature R, making debugging hard. Thus now enabled debugging mode via new argument: use_tryCatch=FALSE.

title: Automated Genomic Fine-Mapping
text: default, echolocatoR::finemap_loci() returns nested list containing grouped locus names (e.g. $BST1, $MEX3C). results locus contain following elements: finemap_dat: Fine-mapping results selected methods merged original summary statistics (.e. Multi-finemap results). locus_plot: nested list containing one zoomed views locus plots. LD_matrix: post-processed LD matrix used fine-mapping. LD_plot: LD plot (used). locus_dir: Locus directory results saved . arguments: record arguments supplied finemap_loci. addition, following object summarizes results locus-specific elements: - merged_dat: merged data.table fine-mapping results loci.

title: Automated Genomic Fine-Mapping
text: main output echolocatoR multi-finemap files (example, echodata::BST1). stored locus-specific Multi-finemap subfolders.

title: Automated Genomic Fine-Mapping
text: Standardized GWAS/QTL summary statistics: e.g. SNP,CHR,POS,Effect,StdErr. See ?finemap_loci() descriptions . leadSNP: designated proxy SNP per locus, SNP smallest p-value default. <tool>.CS: 95% probability Credible Set (CS) SNP belongs within given fine-mapping tool’s results. SNP tool’s CS, assigned NA (0 purposes plotting). <tool>.PP: posterior probability SNP causal given GWAS/QTL trait. Support: total number fine-mapping tools include SNP CS. Consensus_SNP: default, defined SNP included CS N fine-mapping tool(s), .e. Support>1 (default: N=1). mean.PP: mean SNP-wise PP across fine-mapping tools used. mean.CS: mean PP greater 95% probability threshold (mean.PP>0.95) mean.CS 1, else 0. tends stringent threshold requires high degree agreement fine-mapping tools.

title: Automated Genomic Fine-Mapping
text: Separate multi-finemap files generated LD reference panel used, included file name (e.g. UKB_LD.Multi-finemap.tsv.gz). fine-mapping tool defines CS PP slightly differently, please refer associated original publications exact details calculated (links provided ).

title: Automated Genomic Fine-Mapping
text: Fine-mapping functions now implemented via echofinemap: echolocatoR automatically check whether necessary columns run tool selected echolocatoR::finemap_loci(finemap_methods=...). remove tools missing necessary columns, produces message letting know columns missing. Note columns (e.g. MAF,N,t-stat) automatically inferred missing. easy reference, list necessary columns well. See ?echodata::construct_colmap() descriptions columns. methods require columns: SNP,CHR,POS,Effect,StdErr

Also handles semi-automated standardization summary statistics. - echoannot: Annotates GWAS/QTL summary statistics using epigenomics, pre-compiled annotation matrices, machine learning model predictions variant-specific functional impacts. - catalogueR: Large compendium fully standardized e/s/t-QTL summary statistics. detailed information dataset, use ?:

text: queries tabix-indexed files (rapid data subset extraction) implemented via echotabix. echotabix::convert_and_query() detects whether GWAS summary statistics file provided already tabix-indexed, , automatically performs steps necessary convert (sorting, bgzip-compression, indexing) across wide variety scenarios. echotabix::query() contains many different methods making tabix queries (e.g. Rtracklayer,echoconda,VariantAnnotation,seqminer), fail certain circumstances. avoid , query() automatically selects method work particular file queried machine’s particular versions R/Bioconductor/OS, taking guesswork troubleshooting tabix queries.

title: echolocatoR: Automated Genomic Fine-Mapping — echolocatoR-package
text: Automated statistical functional fine-mapping extensive access genome-wide datasets. Automated statistical functional fine-mapping extensive access genome-wide datasets.

title: echolocatoR: Automated Genomic Fine-Mapping — echolocatoR-package
text: Fine-mapping methods powerful means identifying causal variants underlying given phenotype,  underutilized due technical challenges implementation. echolocatoR R package automates end--end genomics fine-mapping, annotation, plotting order identify probable causal variants associated given phenotype. requires minimal input users (GWAS QTL summary statistics file), includes suite statistical functional fine-mapping tools. also includes extensive access datasets (linkage disequilibrium panels, epigenomic genome-wide annotations, QTL). elimination data gathering preprocessing steps enables rapid fine-mapping many loci phenotype,  complete locus-specific publication-ready figure generation.  results merged single per-SNP summary file additional downstream analysis   results sharing. Therefore echolocatoR drastically reduces barriers identifying   causal variants making entire fine-mapping pipeline rapid, robust scalable.
